nameMeanings.name: Robinson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Robin , a pet form of Robert , + -son. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Robin  .

West Indian (including Haiti) and Guyanese: most likely not (only) of English or French origin as in 1 above and 2 above, but also, if not mostly, from the related name of the famous Daniel Defoe's literary character Robinson Crusoe (from a novel first published in 1719).

variants.name: Robbinson
meanings: Altered form of English or French   Robinson  .

variants.name: Robin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Emile, Marcel, Achille, Armand, Arnaud, Camille, Julien, Michel, Normand.
French, Breton, Scottish, English, and German: from the personal name Robin, a pet form of Robert , composed of the short form Rob + the hypocoristic suffix -in.
Slovenian: topographic name derived from rob ‘edge, border’ (see Robar ).

nameMeanings.name: Margarett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margaretha
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: Margarett Robinson was born in 1186, in Preston Patrick, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom. She married Richard Preston about 1207, in Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Margarett Robinson(은)는 1186년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 프레스턴 패트릭에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1207년 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 Richard Preston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.
